section: ABSTRACT
text: This report describes the mathematical model and analog computer program which were used in the preliminary study of the dynamics and control of the 1000-MW(e) single-fluid Molten-Salt Breeder Reactor.

section: ABSTRACT
text: The results and conclusions of the study were reported earlier in Control Studies of a 1000-Mw(e) MSBR, ORNL-TM-2927 (May 18, 1970), by W.

section: 1. INTRODUCTION
text: A preliminary investigation was made of the dynamics and possible control schemes for the proposed 1000-MW(e) single-fluid Molten-Salt Breeder Reactor (MSBR).

section: 1. INTRODUCTION
text: The specific transients investigated using this simulation, the results, and the conclusions were presented in another report.<sup>1</sup>

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: The proposed 1000-MW(e) MSBR steam-electric generating plant consisted of a 2250-MW(t) graphite-moderated molten-salt reactor, 4 shell-and-tube primary heat exchangers, and 16 shell-and-tube supercritical steam generators (Fig.

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: The reactor core contained two zones: a central zone, a cylinder $\sim 14.4$ ft in diameter and $\sim 13$ ft high with a primary-salt volume fraction of 0.132; and an outer zone, an annular region $\sim 1.25$ ft thick and the same height as the central zone.

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: The salt volume fraction in this region was 0.37.

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: The primary salt, bearing $^{233}\mathrm{U}$ and $^{232}\mathrm{Th}$ , flowed upward through the graphite core in a single pass and then to the tube side of one of four vertical single-pass primary heat exchangers, each $\sim 19$ ft long, 5 ft in diameter, and constructed of Hastelloy N.

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: The salt flow rate at design point was $9.48\times 10^7$ lb/hr.

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: The design-point temperature of the salt entering the core was $1050^{\circ}\mathrm{F}$ and that at the core outlet was $1300^{\circ}\mathrm{F}$ .

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: The liquidus temperature of this salt was approximately $930^{\circ}\mathrm{F}$ .

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: Thus there was a total of 16 steam generators in the plant.

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: The design-point flow rate of secondary salt in each loop was $1.78 \times 10^{7} \mathrm{lb/hr}$ .

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: At the design point the secondary-salt cold-leg temperature was $850^{\circ}\mathrm{F}$ , and the hot-leg temperature was $1150^{\circ}\mathrm{F}$ .

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: The liquidus temperature of this salt was $\sim 725^{\circ}\mathrm{F}$ .

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: The shell-and-tube supercritical steam generators were countercurrent single-pass U-tube exchangers $\sim 73$ ft long and $\sim 18$ in. in diameter and were constructed of Hastelloy N.

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: Feedwater entered the steam generators at the design point at $700^{\circ}\mathrm{F}$ and a pressure of about 3750 psi.

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: The outlet steam conditions at the design point were $1000^{\circ}\mathrm{F}$ and 3600 psi.

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: Each steam generator produced steam at the design point at a rate of $6.30 \times 10^{5} \mathrm{lb/hr}$ .

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: Reference 2 gives a complete description of an earlier, but quite similar, version of the steam generator and primary heat exchanger.

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: The load control system used in this study maintained the temperature of the steam delivered to the turbines at a design value of $1000^{\circ}\mathrm{F}$ during all steady-state conditions and within a narrow band around this value during plant transients.

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: It consisted of a reactor outlet temperature controller similar to that used successfully in the $\mathrm{MSRE}^3$ and a steam temperature controller.

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: The measured steam temperature was compared with its set point of $1000^{\circ}\mathrm{F}$ , and any error caused the secondary-salt flow rate to change at a rate proportional to the error if the error was $2^{\circ}\mathrm{F}$ or less.

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: If the error was greater than $2^{\circ}\mathrm{F}$ , the rate of change of the secondary-salt flow rate was limited to its rate of change for a $2^{\circ}\mathrm{F}$ error, which was approximately $11\%/\mathrm{min}$ .

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: The outlet temperature set point was a linear function of load demand, varying between 1125 and $1300^{\circ}\mathrm{F}$ for loads above $50\%$ and between 1000 and $1125^{\circ}\mathrm{F}$ for loads below $50\%$ .

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: Under normal conditions, the control rod servo added or removed reactivity at a rate proportional to the reactor power error if the error was $1\%$ or less.

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: If the error was greater than $1\%$ , the addition or removal rate was limited to the rate for a $1\%$ error, which was about $0.01\%/\mathrm{sec}$ .

section: 2. DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT AND CONTROL SYSTEM
text: The maximum magnitude of reactivity that the simulation allowed was $\pm 1\%$ .

section: 3.1 Model
text: 3) consisted of the reactor core, one primary heat exchanger, one steam generator, the nuclear kinetics, and a control system as shown in Fig.

section: 3.1 Model
text: All 4 primary heat exchangers were combined into 1 and all 16 steam generators into 1.

section: 3.1 Model
text: A two-delayed-neutron-group approximation of the circulating-fuel nuclear kinetics equations<sup>4</sup> was used in the model.

section: 3.1 Model
text: 3.2.1) to be simulated directly with two of four available transport lag devices.

section: 3.1 Model
text: The delayed-neutron fraction for $^{233}\mathrm{U}$ was 0.00264, and the prompt-neutron generation time was 0.36 msec.

section: 3.1 Model
text: The coefficient of reactivity for the primary salt was $-1.33 \times 10^{-5} \delta k / k$ per $^{\circ}\mathrm{F}$ , which was divided equally among the six primary-salt lumps of the core model.

section: 3.1 Model
text: The temperature coefficient for the graphite was $+1.06 \times 10^{-5} \delta k / k$ per $^{\circ}\mathrm{F}$ , which was divided equally among the three graphite lumps.

section: 3.1 Model
text: The film coefficient for secondary salt on the shell side of the primary heat exchanger and steam generator was proportional to the 0.6 power of the flow rate.

section: 3.1 Model
text: The film coefficient for steam on the tube side of the steam generators was assumed to be proportional to the 0.8 power of the flow rate.

section: 3.1 Model
text: Under design conditions the supercritical steam density varied from $34\mathrm{lb / ft}^3$ at the feedwater inlet to $5\mathrm{lb / ft}^3$ at the steam outlet.[6] The density of the steam in the lump nearest the feedwater entrance was taken as the average density in the quarter of the steam generator represented by that lump, or $22.7\mathrm{lb / ft}^3$ .
